meaning: adj.颠覆性的,破坏性的n.颠覆破坏分子,危险分子
examples: Attempting to resolve this contradiction, Harris concludes that Fern employed flowery rhetoric strategically to disguise her subversive goals beneath apparent conventionality.
Augustine found this doctrine subversive and distasteful.
Sometimes they were subversive enemies of the Roman order.
Like his previous best sellers, it presents a familiar story with a subversive twist.
Until home brewing was legalised in the US in 1979, enthusiasts considered themselves vaguely subversive.
